subject: Ivanka Trump and Apple CEO Tim Cook pay students a visit
body:
Yesterday, students at Wilder Elementary School in Idaho lined up in anticipation for two very special guests...

https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=fbf5919f9f&e=3ee4985eea
The White House • November 28, 2018

** Ivanka Trump teams up with Apple CEO Tim Cook
------------------------------------------------------------
Yesterday, students at Wilder Elementary School in Idaho lined up in anticipation for two very special guests: Apple CEO Tim Cook and Advisor to the President Ivanka Trump (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2bc9b549a7&e=3ee4985eea) . With iPads in hand, the students got a chance to show off their tech prowess.

The goal of the visit was simple: to see firsthand how American companies like Apple are transforming classrooms across the country. By incorporating handheld technology, the Wilder School District is personalizing the learning process for students, tailoring the instructional environment to individual strengths and needs.

“Instead of a teacher standing before the entire class and lecturing, the students at Wilder hold the classroom in their hands and complete the work at their own pace,” Cynthia Sewell writes in the Idaho Statesman.

Great teachers remain as important as ever in the age of high-tech classrooms. “This is what is so exciting, the harnessing of technology in conjunction with incredible educators to create this type of really personalized learning experience,” Ms. Trump said yesterday. Such programs “prepare students for a world where digital literacy is absolutely critical.”

This summer, President Donald J. Trump unveiled his “Pledge to America’s Workers” and established the National Council for the American Worker. Spearheaded by Ivanka Trump, the Council is working to develop a strategy that will help train America’s students for the jobs of the future. Apple was among the first companies to sign the pledge committing 10,000 new training opportunities for workers.

Why Ivanka Trump and Tim Cook visited Idaho (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=895609ee44&e=3ee4985eea)

The pledge: More than 6 million opportunities for American workers (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b8d50bfca5&e=3ee4985eea)

** Migrant caravan: How we got here
------------------------------------------------------------
Over the weekend, a group of more than 1,000 migrants—the vast majority of them single adult males—attempted to illegally rush America’s southern border. “People started throwing rocks. Assaulting our agents,” U.S. Customs and Border Protection Commissioner Kevin McAleenan said.

Law enforcement responded professionally to the situation and dispersed the crowd before more serious injuries were sustained. How did we get here?

One important fact looms large (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f1d621bfb2&e=3ee4985eea) : In the past 5 years, the number of "credible fear" claims made by illegal immigrants crossing our southern border has multiplied tenfold. As the number of asylum claims grows, the backlog in our immigration courts has more than tripled in the past decade.

Furthermore, well-known immigration loopholes accommodate and even incentivize fraudulent family relationship claims among migrants. When that happens, children face a frighteningly high risk of exploitation by human traffickers and smugglers.

The bottom line: It's time for Congress to fund real border security, letting immigration courts address the cases of asylum-seekers who present themselves lawfully at our Nation's Ports of Entry.

See President Trump’s 4-part plan to fix America's immigration system. (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c221f83a5e&e=3ee4985eea)

More: 110 percent surge in migrant men using kids to enter the U.S. (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c12497f44a&e=3ee4985eea)

** Photo of the Day
------------------------------------------------------------
Photo Courtesy of Apple
Senior Adviser to the President Ivanka Trump and Apple CEO Tim Cook visit the Wilder School District in Idaho | November 27, 2018

### [Confrontation at the Border](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtfplt-kujkulhluu-i/)
Thousands of mostly young males from Honduras, Guatemala, and El Salvador, have joined together in caravans and are trying to get into our country through our southern border. Yes, there are some women and children too, and they are inevitably the ones CNN and others interview, and put in their news coverage of unfolding events. But most of the people coming are young, and male, and without accompanying children. [Read this entry >>](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtfplt-kujkulhluu-d/) _Previous Weekly Posts_ [](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtfplt-kujkulhluu-h/) [](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtfplt-kujkulhluu-k/)

Well I'm back in Washington, after a month or so being home in Cincinnati, preparing for, and recovering from, the recent election. Again, thank you for having the confidence in me to represent your best interests in the swamp that is Washington nowadays. I'm girding up for battle in what will most certainly be a very different environment - at least in the House. subject: The Trump admin released a report, here’s what it means for you

body:
http://lipinskiforcongress.com/ [http://lipinskiforcongress.com/]
Peter,
In case you missed it, the Trump administration released the Fourth National Climate Assessment report last Friday, and the results are far worse than we could have anticipated.
The Trump administration’s own report found that climate change is already destroying natural resources, agriculture, energy, and human health across the US and its territories, and that we need to take immediate action on global warming to avoid devastating consequences.
We need your help. The Trump administration is refusing to accept the conclusions of its own report, and we need to prove that there’s public support on our side. Add your name if you agree that the government should be doing more to combat climate change → [https://secure.ngpvan.com/QywLWARwMU2xqNQFIDHLvQ2]
The day the report was released, Donald Trump announced that he didn’t accept its conclusions, and refused to acknowledge the devastating effects that climate change can have on our health, our environment, and our economy.
Protecting our environment and combating climate change should not be a partisan issue. We need your name to prove that the American people expect more action. Sign now → [https://secure.ngpvan.com/QywLWARwMU2xqNQFIDHLvQ2]
Thanks,
Dan

Sign now → [] Thanks, Dan Paid for by Dan Lipinski for CongressContributions or gifts to Dan Lipinski for Congress are not tax deductible.

name: DWS (Congresswoman)
subject: Don't let fear win
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress
body:
|

| | President Trump does a lot of fear-mongering. He irresponsibly sent troops to intercept a caravan of immigrants at our southern border. He's hit the TV, purposely spreading lies about voter fraud. And he pits American vs. American, all with the intention of making us afraid of one another. Trump wants us to be afraid. Division helps him. **But we can choose to not let the fear win.** I'm not afraid of Trump, his special interest friends, or his allies in Congress. I'm one of their most outspoken opponents and I'm proud of that. This problem didn't go away on Election Day -- our efforts can't go away, either. [**Our goal for November is $8,000. Can you chip in to help us get there?**](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113cc0/622b7b5b/1e39390f/307b7ead/4131337720/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dwse181129am?refcode=dwse181129am_m&recurring=true&amount=5") _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ **[Express Donate: $5](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113cc0/622b7b5b/1e39390f/307b7eac/4131337720/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VEpTVT0iOiI4NjE3NjkiLCJlM3R6YkhWbmZYMD0iOiJkd3NlMTgxMTI5YW0iLCJlM3RoYlc5MWJuUjlmUT09IjoiNSIsImUzdDJaWEp6YVc5dWZYMD0iOiJtIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dwse181129am?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=dwse181129am_m_5&refcode2=861769")** **[Express Donate: $10](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113cc0/622b7b5b/1e39390f/307b7eac/4131337720/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VEpTVT0iOiI4NjE3NjkiLCJlM3R6YkhWbmZYMD0iOiJkd3NlMTgxMTI5YW0iLCJlM3RoYlc5MWJuUjlmUT09IjoiMTAiLCJlM3QyWlhKemFXOXVmWDA9IjoibSJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dwse181129am?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=dwse181129am_m_10&refcode2=861769")** **[Express Donate: $25](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113cc0/622b7b5b/1e39390f/307b7eac/4131337720/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VEpTVT0iOiI4NjE3NjkiLCJlM3R6YkhWbmZYMD0iOiJkd3NlMTgxMTI5YW0iLCJlM3RoYlc5MWJuUjlmUT09IjoiMjUiLCJlM3QyWlhKemFXOXVmWDA9IjoibSJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dwse181129am?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=dwse181129am_m_25&refcode2=861769")** **[Or donate another amount.](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113cc0/622b7b5b/1e39390f/307b7eaf/4131337720/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VEpTVT0iOiI4NjE3NjkiLCJlM3R6YkhWbmZYMD0iOiJkd3NlMTgxMTI5YW0iLCJlM3QyWlhKemFXOXVmWDA9IjoibSJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dwse181129am?refcode=dwse181129am_m_other&refcode2=861769")** Every contribution, no matter the size, represents an expression of hope. Not just for immigrants on the border, but our immigrant neighbors and friends. Not just for seniors across the country, but the seniors in our own families. [**Give what you can before Friday's deadline.**](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113cc0/622b7b5b/1e39390f/307b7ead/4131337720/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dwse181129am?refcode=dwse181129am_m&recurring=true&amount=5") And please know I appreciate all of your help. name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: President Trump, First Lady Participate in Tree Lighting Ceremony
body:
President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump “participated in the 96th National Christmas Tree Lighting ceremony... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=eff62f511f&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **President Trump, First Lady Participate in Tree Lighting Ceremony**

President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump “participated in the 96th National Christmas Tree Lighting ceremony on the Ellipse outside the White House on Wednesday, with the White House grounds fully decked out for the holidays,” CBS News reports.

“The president had the crowd count down to the tree lighting with him. The tradition draws hundreds of spectators each year, even in frigid conditions.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3315750a08&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “Consumers boosted their spending in October at the fastest pace in seven months, while their incomes rose by the largest amount in nine months — both good signs for future economic growth,” Martin Crutsinger reports for _[The Associated Press](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=bfed0f5ff1&e=3ee4985eea)_. “Consumer spending rose a sharp 0.6 percent last month, the Commerce Department reported Thursday. It was the biggest increase since a similar gain in March.”
---
|
---
| The Tax Cuts and Jobs Act signed by President Trump last year “continues to encourage the business investment that leads to higher productivity and higher wages for American workers. Today the government reported that such investment was higher than it initially reported for the third quarter of the year,” James Freeman writes in _[The Wall Street Journal](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=73bc4f68ef&e=3ee4985eea)_. As the economy continues to boom, it’s time to “get ready for more speeches featuring America’s 44th President [Barack Obama] asserting ownership of the growth breakout that never managed to arrive during his eight years in the White House.”
---
|
---
| “First lady Melania Trump spoke about the importance of raising awareness about the opioid crisis and ending the stigma associated with addiction Wednesday,” Megan Keller reports in _[The Hill](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f442bb9069&e=3ee4985eea)_. “The chances of you knowing someone who struggles with it are very high,” the First Lady said during a town hall at Liberty University.
---
|
---
| “A self-identified member of the violent gang MS-13 was arrested on Saturday, telling authorities that he traveled to the United States with the migrant caravan,” Leah Barkoukis reports for _[Townhall](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f89763d1b1&e=3ee4985eea)_. The 29-year-old told authorities he traveled to the U.S. border with a large group of people from Central America with plans to file for asylum. “As President Trump and Secretary Nielsen warned, over the last few weeks we have identified over 600 known criminals and gang members in the caravan,” the Department of Homeland Security said in a statement.
---
|

name: Jay Inslee
subject: GET. THINGS. DONE.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
But we can't rest now.

| [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7b3aa/2f5ea88d/7b03b568/2cbdaf91/1138550323/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/inslee-freeze?utm_source=ema_2018-11-28_eom-1&refcode=ema_2018-11-28_eom-1&utm_campaign=eom-1&utm_medium=ema") Friend, It's been a big month for us. We watched as Democrats swept governors' offices across the country and took the House. And in my home state, we made significant gains towards a more progressive legislature ready to take action for Washington families. Friend, we are going to get big things done in 2019, 2020, and beyond, but many Republicans will fight our progress every step of the way. That is why it is critical we keep up our political power as we head into the legislative session. [**So we're setting a big fundraising goal of $20,000 before November 30th to show _everyone_ that We. Mean. name: Shailene Woodley, Our Revolution
subject: What do climate and immigration have to do with each other?
body:
Two of the largest humanitarian and environmental challenges facing our nation are inextricably linked.
#
Friends,
As I'm writing to you, California is experiencing refugee crises at both ends of the state. At the southern border, families are seeking asylum from violence and poverty. They are sheltering in tents and being tear gassed by U.S. officials. In northern California, thousands of people who lost their homes in the Camp Fire are now living in tents and in Walmart parking lots, without adequate housing to meet the scale of this disaster.
Two of the largest humanitarian and environmental challenges facing our nation are inextricably linked. We cannot continue to ignore the devastating effects of climate change, which is causing thousands of people in Central America to flee their homes. Climate change will soon drive human migration more than any other event.
Our common humanity and our planet require compassionate immigration policies, a Green New Deal, and leaders who will do the right thing. People like you can unite our country and stop those who seek to divide us by taking action to create a better world. That mission is what Our Revolution is here to support.
**[Over 600 local groups across the country and around the globe are organizing our communities to build the progressive movement, elect progressive candidates, and transform the Democratic Party. Can you pitch in $5 to Our Revolution's grassroots organizing efforts today, and help us to ensure that everyone has a voice in shaping the future of our country?](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a73c/bed43eb/8a1967e3/75fc5ee7/323524795/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/radical-change?refcode=em181129-full&noskip=true")**
You have proven that real change doesn't need to come from the top down - local groups have brought it from the grassroots to Congress, state capitols and city halls. Leaders like Congresswomen-elect Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, Rashida Tlaib, Deb Haaland, Ilhan Omar and many more are speaking truth to power and challenging the established power structures. We are ready to go further.
We can make radical, positive change right now. Comprehensive immigration reform, with a pathway to citizenship for undocumented people in our country and 100% clean energy in time to save our planet from climate catastrophe - these things are possible, but not without each one of us getting involved. In the next two years, we must do everything in our power to affect this change.
**[Your support is critical to Our Revolution's mission of building a progressive movement across this country. Can you make a $5 contribution to Our Revolution's organizing efforts today?](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a73c/bed43eb/8a1967e3/75fc5ee7/323524795/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/radical-change?refcode=em181129-full&noskip=true")**
In years of organizing, what you have taught me is that nothing big gets done without people willing to fight for it. From calls to Congress, rallies and meetings, and the contact from one person to another, you are the power to change the world.
In solidarity,
Shailene Woodley
Our Revolution Board Member

subject: It’s lit!
body:
Last night, President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump celebrated a classic America tradition: the lighting of the National Christmas Tree

https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=087a4f8941&e=3ee4985eea
The White House • November 29, 2018

** The 96th lighting of the National Christmas Tree
------------------------------------------------------------
Last night, President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump celebrated a classic America tradition: the lighting of the National Christmas Tree.

“This is a time of the year for rejoicing with our family and friends, for spreading charity and goodwill in our commitment all over this country in all of our communities, and for giving thanks for all of the blessings in our lives,” the President said from the Ellipse at President’s Park, just steps from the White House.

“No matter one’s faith or beliefs, the Christmas season reflects all that is best in the American spirit.”

Every year, crowds withstand the sometimes frigid D.C. temperatures to witness the ceremony put on by the National Park Service and the National Park Foundation. Amid multiple musical performances by a range of guests, including the U.S. Army Band, the President and First Lady led a countdown to the tree’s first lighting of the season.

“On behalf of Melania and myself and the entire Trump family—many of whom are with us tonight—I want to again wish you all a very, very Merry Christmas.”

Watch President Trump and the First Lady lead the National Christmas Tree lighting. (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7e1f096e68&e=3ee4985eea)

More: The First Lady unveils this year’s White House Christmas decorations (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2e6bb3adcd&e=3ee4985eea)

** First Lady Melania Trump talks opioid abuse at Liberty University
------------------------------------------------------------
Yesterday morning, First Lady Melania Trump joined Eric Bolling (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=06dbe45a8f&e=3ee4985eea) , Health and Human Services Secretary Alex Azar, and Homeland Security Secretary Kirstjen Nielsen at Liberty University in Virginia for a town hall on opioids.

“When I took on opioid abuse as one of the pillars of my initiative BeBest, I did it with the goal of helping children of all ages,” the First Lady said.

To a full stadium of students, she explained the scale of the problem and how each person can make a difference. More than 130 Americans die each day from opioid overdoses. Last year, those overdoses accounted for over 72,000 deaths—more than any previous year on record.

Under President Trump’s leadership, signs of hope are emerging. “The results speak for themselves,” Counselor to the President Kellyanne Conway wrote last month. “Seizures of several kinds of illicit drugs are up, the number of new 30-day prescriptions is down and overdose deaths attributed to prescription pain medications have started to level.”

Watch highlights of the First Lady’s talk at Liberty University. (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=44c7c64567&e=3ee4985eea)

Kellyanne Conway: We can bring the opioid crisis to an end (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e68b0232a5&e=3ee4985eea)

------------------------------------------------------------
